---
title: "Week 9 Milestone Tables"
author: "<img src='images/logo.png' width='50' /> <br> Dairy Cow Foot Doc"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
    theme: flatly
    df_print: paged
    toc: true
    toc-location: left
    number-sections: false
    code-fold: true
execute:
  message: false
  warning: false
  echo: false
embed-resources: true
params:
  event:
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false

if (!require("pacman")) install.packages("pacman")
pacman::p_load(
  broom,
  tidyverse,
  dtplyr,
  gt,
  gtExtras,
  arrow,
  rmarkdown,
  lubridate,
  quarto, 
  arrow,
  survival,
  survminer,
  ggsurvfit,
  flextable,
  gtsummary,
  marginaleffects,
  scales,
  zoo
  )

events_formatted <- read_parquet(
  '../data/intermediate_files/events_formatted.parquet') |> 
  mutate(location_event = ifelse(location_event == "Herd MMW10", "MMW Dairy", "MV Dairy"))

# events_formatted <- events_formatted |>
#   dplyr::mutate(
#     location_event = ifelse(
#       source_file_path == "data/event_files/MV Dairy 1.16.26.csv",
#       "MV Dairy",
#       "MMW Dairy"
#     )
#   )

animals <- read_parquet(
  '../data/intermediate_files/animals.parquet')

# animals <- animals |>
#   dplyr::mutate(
#     location_list = ifelse(
#       grepl("MV Dairy", id_animal, ignore.case = TRUE),
#       "MV Dairy",
#       "MMW Dairy"
#     )
#   )

deno_cal <- read_parquet(
  '../data/intermediate_files/denominator_by_calendar_time_period.parquet') |> 
  filter(deno_type == "lact_basic" & 
           calendar_time_period_type == "year") |> 
  select(-c(contains("dop"), day_of_phase_group))

deno_cal <- deno_cal |> 
  mutate(
    location_lact_list = ifelse(
      grepl("MV", location_lact_list, ignore.case = TRUE),
      "MV Dairy",
      "MMW Dairy"
    )
  )
  
# load GC's functions
source('../functions/fxn_load_os_fxns.R')
fxn_load_os_fxns()

# Load Parnell CR fucntions from Github
cr_url <- "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ParnellPharma/ParnellFunctionsPublic/main/parnell_calculate_CR_master.R"
source(cr_url)

# create variables for inline code
farm_name <- unique(events_formatted$location_event)

#date_max_pull <- max(events_formatted$date_event, na.rm = TRUE)
date_max_pull <- events_formatted %>%
  filter(location_event == "MMW Dairy") %>%
  summarise(date_max_pull = max(date_event, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  pull(date_max_pull)
date_min_pull <- date_max_pull - years(1)

date_max_pull

```


# Report for `r farm_name[[1]]` and `r farm_name[[2]]`

## Objective

Run this chuck below. It will open the example report in your browser.
You should delete this chunk in your final report

```{r}
#| label: open-html-r
#| eval: false

# 1. Define the file path
file_path <- "images/milestone_09.htm"

# 2. Open the browser
browseURL(file_path)

```


The objective of this milestone is start working with multiple herds, create tables, use the denominator data and use external functions. The data and functions you need are loaded in the top chunk. This is in prep for milestone 10 where we will use parameters to run the same report for multiple herds.

The steps for this milestone are as follows (code sections for these steps are below)

1.  Re-Run **step0_master_processing.R**
2.  Describe the herd size for the past 5 years for the 2 herds
3.  Run the parnell CR function, by calling **fxn_create_CR_reports**
    1.  More details about the function can be found [here](https://github.com/ParnellPharma/ParnellFunctionsPublic/blob/main/parnell_calculate_CR_master.R)
4.  Run the **fxn_dz_status** as it relates to mastitis to determine if type early mastitis case influences conception risk.
5.  Create tables from the inventory and both conception risk data frames.

## Describe Herds

-   Using the deno_cal dataframe recreate this table using the gt table skills you learned this week. You will probably need to look at the package website or use AI to get some of the details.

-   Some gt function hints are:

    -   groupname_col and rowname_col inside gt()

    -   fmt_data

    -   fmt_number

    -   tab_style

    -   cols_label

    -   tab_spanner

    -   tab_options

```{r}

deno_5y_tbl <- deno_cal %>%
  filter(calendar_time_period_type == "year") %>%
  filter(deno_type == "lact_basic") %>%
  mutate(
    Year = year(date_time_period_end),
    Lactation = ifelse(`Lactation Group` == "LACT > 0", "Adult Cows", "Heifer")
  ) %>%
  filter(Year >= max(Year, na.rm = TRUE) - 4) %>%
  select(
    location_event = location_lact_list,
    Year,
    Lactation,
    `Days at risk` = ct_animal_days_elig, 
    `DAR Animals`  = ct_animals,      
    Lactations     = ct_animal_lactations,
    Animals        = ct_animals
  ) %>%
  arrange(location_event, desc(Year), Lactation)

gt_herd_desc <- deno_5y_tbl %>%
  gt(
    groupname_col = "location_event",
    rowname_col = "Year"
  ) %>%
  tab_spanner(
    label = "Count of",
    columns = c(`Days at risk`, `DAR Animals`, Lactations, Animals)
  ) %>%
  tab_spanner(
    label = "Group",
    columns = c(Lactation)
  ) %>%
  fmt_number(
    columns = c(`Days at risk`, `DAR Animals`, Lactations, Animals),
    decimals = 0,
    use_seps = TRUE
  ) %>%
  fmt_number(
    rows = everything(),
    columns = stub(), 
    decimals = 0,
    use_seps = FALSE
  ) %>%
  tab_style(
    style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
    locations = list(cells_column_labels(), cells_row_groups())
  ) %>%
  tab_options(
    row_group.background.color = "#eeeeee",
    table.width = pct(100),
    table.font.size = px(14)
  ) %>%
  cols_align(align = "center", columns = everything())

gt_herd_desc

```

## Conception Risk Tables

Use the Parnell CR function to compare the conception risks between the 2 herds and recreate this table

1.  The **fxn_create_CR_reports** function will work if you just pipe the events_formatted dataframe to it.
2.  You will need define the grp_vars inside the function

-   the last one should be Rstandard
-   the table uses lact_group_basic as one of the variables to group the data

3.  for the table use some of the same gt function you used in describing the herd sizes.

-   additional functions to explore are:
-   summary_rows
-   cols_nanoplot \_ cols_merge_range

```{r}
#| label: create-cr_table

events_formatted <- events_formatted |> 
  filter(date_event <= date_max_pull) 

cr_df <- events_formatted %>%
  mutate(year = year(date_event)) %>%
  fxn_create_CR_reports(
    grp_vars = c("location_event", "year", "lact_group_basic", "Rstandard")
  )

cr_table <- cr_df %>%
  mutate(
    Risk_Num = CR * 100,
    Plot_Data = CR * 100
  ) %>% 
  select(
    location_event, 
    Year = year, 
    Lactation = lact_group_basic, 
    `Not Bred Event`, 
    Preg = preg, 
    Total = total, 
    SPC, 
    Risk = Risk_Num, 
    CI_lower, 
    CI_upper,
    Plot_Data 
  ) %>%
  gt(groupname_col = "location_event") %>%
  
  fmt_number(columns = c(`Not Bred Event`, Preg, Total), decimals = 0, use_seps = TRUE) %>%
  fmt_number(columns = c(SPC, Risk), decimals = 1) %>%
  
  cols_merge_range(col_begin = CI_lower, col_end = CI_upper, sep = "–") %>%
  cols_nanoplot(
    columns = Plot_Data,
    plot_type = "bar",
    new_col_name = "Plot_Col",
    options = nanoplot_options(
      data_bar_fill = "orange",
      data_bar_stroke_color = "black",
      data_bar_stroke_width = 1
    )
  ) %>%
  cols_label(CI_lower = "95% CI", Plot_Col = "Plot") %>%
  
  summary_rows(
    groups = everything(), # Force summary on all groups
    columns = Risk,
    fns = list("Location Average" = ~mean(., na.rm = TRUE)),
    fmt = list(~fmt_number(., decimals = 1)),
    missing_text = "—" 
  ) %>%
  
  tab_header(title = "Conception Risk Report") %>%
  tab_spanner(label = "Conception (%)", columns = c(Risk, CI_lower, Plot_Col)) %>%
  tab_options(
    summary_row.background.color = "lightblue",
    row_group.font.weight = "bold",
    column_labels.font.weight = "bold",
    table.width = pct(100)
  ) %>%
  cols_align(align = "center", columns = everything())

cr_table

```

## Mastitis and Conception Risk

Now recreate the table low were a variable was created to determine if a cow had mastitis in her first 60 DIM.

1.  Once you create the new mastitis variable then run the **fxn_dz_status**

    -   The gap should be 14 days

    -   If the cow had mastitis in the first 60 DIM then she will be classified as

        -   "New" if it was her first case

        -   "Repeat" if it was a subsequent case within the gap

        -   "Chronic" if she had at least 1 case after the gap

2.  After running this function

    -   assign cows that had mastitis \>60 DIM as "After"

3.  Merge the data back with events formatted.

    -   I have provided some code below to help you get started.

    -   After the join, cows with no mastitis events should be coded as No Mast

4.  After you merged the mastitis status variable back into events_formatted run the **fxn_create_CR_reports** function again to get the conception risk by mastitis status

5.  Then create a table to show the results

```{r}
#| label: mast

events_prep <- events_formatted %>%
  mutate(mast_60 = ifelse(event == "MAST" & dim_event <= 60, 1, 0)) %>%
  select(-any_of("id_animal")) 

mastitis_status <- fxn_dz_status(
    data = events_prep,
    farm_col = location_event,
    id_col = id_animal_lact, 
    disease_cols = "mast_60",
    prefix = "mast_60",
    gap = 14
  )

mastitis_refined <- mastitis_status %>%
  rename(
    life_times_mast_60 = life_times_mast_60.x,
  ) %>%
  filter(mast_60 == 1) %>% 
  group_by(location_event, id_animal, lact_number) %>%
  slice_max(order_by = life_times_mast_60, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(location_event, id_animal_lact = id_animal, lact_number, early_status = status_mast_60)

events_mast <- events_formatted %>%
  left_join(mastitis_refined, by = c("location_event", "id_animal_lact", "lact_number")) %>%
  mutate(
    has_late_mast = any(event == "MAST" & dim_event > 60), .by = c(location_event, id_animal_lact, lact_number)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    mast_status = case_when(
      !is.na(early_status) ~ early_status, # "New", "Repeat", "Chronic" (from function)
      has_late_mast == TRUE ~ "After",     # Mastitis > 60 DIM
      TRUE ~ "No Mast"                     # No mastitis events at all
    )
  )

cr_mast_df <- events_mast %>%
  mutate(
    year = year(date_event),
  ) %>%
  filter(year >= 2023) %>% 
  fxn_create_CR_reports(
    grp_vars = c("location_event", "year", "mast_status", "Rstandard")
  )

mast_cr_table <- cr_mast_df %>%
  mutate(
    Plot_Data = CR * 100, 
    Risk_Num = CR * 100
  ) %>%
  select(
    location_event,
    Year = year,
    `Mast <= 60 DIM` = mast_status,
    Preg = preg,
    Total = total,
    Risk = Risk_Num,
    CI_lower,
    CI_upper,
    Plot_Data
  ) %>%
  gt(groupname_col = "location_event") %>%
  
  fmt_number(columns = c(Preg, Total), decimals = 0, use_seps = TRUE) %>%
  fmt_number(columns = Risk, decimals = 1) %>%
  
  cols_merge_range(col_begin = CI_lower, col_end = CI_upper, sep = "–") %>%
  
  cols_nanoplot(
    columns = Plot_Data,
    plot_type = "bar",
    new_col_name = "Plot_Col",
    options = nanoplot_options(
      data_bar_fill = "orange",
      data_bar_stroke_color = "black",
      data_bar_stroke_width = 1
    )
  ) %>%

  cols_label(
    CI_lower = "95% CI", 
    Plot_Col = "Plot",
    Risk = "Risk"
  ) %>%
  tab_spanner(
    label = "Conception (%)", 
    columns = c(Risk, CI_lower, Plot_Col)
  ) %>%
  
  tab_source_note(source_note = md(
    "**After** = Mastitis after 60 days in milk<br>
     **New** = First case<br>
     **Repeat** = Multiple cases in gap<br>
     **Chronic** = At least 1 case after gap<br>
     **No Mast** = No mastitis cases in lactation"
  )) %>%
  
  tab_style(
    style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
    locations = list(cells_column_labels(), cells_row_groups())
  ) %>%
  
  tab_options(
    row_group.background.color = "#eeeeee", # Grey bars for Herd names
    table.width = pct(100),
    table.font.size = px(14),
    data_row.padding = px(5)
  ) %>%
  cols_align(align = "center", columns = everything())

mast_cr_table
```

# Extension

For this week extension continue to create standard things you would want to report or incorporate modeling techniques to explore the data

Ideas

-   Try the code with other diseases like mastitis or culture information


```{r}
#| label: metr
events_metr_prep <- events_formatted %>%
  filter(event == "METR") %>%
  mutate(metr_60 = ifelse(dim_event <= 60, 1, 0)) |> 
  select(-any_of("id_animal"))
  
metritis_status <- fxn_dz_status(
    data = events_metr_prep,
    farm_col = location_event,
    id_col = id_animal_lact, 
    disease_cols = "metr_60",
    prefix = "metr_60",
    gap = 14
  )

metritis_refined <- metritis_status %>%
  rename(life_times_metr_60 = life_times_metr_60.x) %>%
  # Only look at the early window
  filter(metr_60 == 1) %>%
  group_by(location_event, id_animal, lact_number) %>%
  # Use slice_min to get the protocol from the VERY FIRST metritis event
  slice_min(order_by = life_times_metr_60, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(
    location_event, 
    id_animal_lact = id_animal, 
    lact_number, 
    early_protocol = protocols_letters1 
  )

events_metr_final <- events_formatted %>%
  left_join(metritis_refined, by = c("location_event", "id_animal_lact", "lact_number")) %>%
  mutate(
    # Check for metritis > 60 DIM for cows not in the early_protocol group
    has_late_metr = any(event == "METR" & dim_event > 60), .by = c(location_event, id_animal_lact, lact_number)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    # Create the final variable for the table
    metr_protocol_grp = case_when(
      !is.na(early_protocol) ~ early_protocol, # The letters (A, B, etc.)
      has_late_metr == TRUE ~ "After",
      TRUE ~ "No Metritis"
    )
  )

cr_metr_df <- events_metr_final %>%
  mutate(year = year(date_event)) %>%
  filter(year >= 2023) %>% 
  fxn_create_CR_reports(
    # IMPORTANT: Use metr_protocol_grp here, NOT Rstandard
    grp_vars = c("location_event", "year", "metr_protocol_grp", "Rstandard")
  )

metr_proto_table <- cr_metr_df %>%
  mutate(Plot_Data = CR * 100, Risk_Num = CR * 100) %>%
  select(
    location_event,
    Year = year,
    `Metritis Protocol` = metr_protocol_grp,
    Preg = preg,
    Total = total,
    Risk = Risk_Num,
    CI_lower,
    CI_upper,
    Plot_Data
  ) %>%
  gt(groupname_col = "location_event") %>%
  
  # Formatting
  fmt_number(columns = c(Preg, Total), decimals = 0, use_seps = TRUE) %>%
  fmt_number(columns = Risk, decimals = 1) %>%
  cols_merge_range(col_begin = CI_lower, col_end = CI_upper, sep = "–") %>%
  
  # Nanoplots (using a different color to distinguish from Mastitis)
  cols_nanoplot(
    columns = Plot_Data,
    plot_type = "bar",
    new_col_name = "Plot_Col",
    options = nanoplot_options(
      data_bar_fill = "darkorchid",
      data_bar_stroke_color = "black",
      data_bar_stroke_width = 1
    )
  ) %>%
  
  # Labels and Spanners
  cols_label(CI_lower = "95% CI", Plot_Col = "Plot") %>%
  tab_spanner(label = "Conception (%)", columns = c(Risk, CI_lower, Plot_Col)) %>%
  
  # Aesthetics
  tab_options(
    row_group.background.color = "#eeeeee",
    row_group.font.weight = "bold",
    table.width = pct(100)
  ) %>%
  cols_align(align = "center", columns = everything())

metr_proto_table
```
```{r}
#| label: mast survivability

# 1. CALCULATE SURVIVAL FROM CALVING (ECONOMIC IMPACT)
# ---------------------------------------------------------
mast_survival_econ <- events_mast %>%
  # Create the lactation groups first
  mutate(lact_group = case_when(
    lact_number == 1 ~ "1st Lact",
    lact_number == 2 ~ "2nd Lact",
    lact_number >= 3 ~ "3+ Lact",
    TRUE ~ "Other"
  )) %>%
  group_by(location_event, id_animal_lact, lact_number) %>%
  mutate(
    calving_date = min(date_event),
    is_exit = ifelse(any(event %in% c("SOLD", "DIED")), 1, 0),
    exit_date = max(date_event),
    total_days_in_lact = as.numeric(exit_date - calving_date)
  ) %>%
  slice(1) %>%
  ungroup()

# 2. SUMMARIZE BY STATUS AND LACT_GROUP
# ---------------------------------------------------------
mast_econ_summary <- mast_survival_econ %>%
  filter(year(date_event) >= 2023) %>%
  group_by(location_event, lact_group, mast_status) %>% # Added lact_group here
  summarise(
    Cow_Count = n(),
    Culling_Rate = mean(is_exit) * 100,
    Avg_Lactation_Length = mean(total_days_in_lact, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# 3. GENERATE THE UPDATED GT TABLE
# ---------------------------------------------------------
mast_econ_table_lact <- mast_econ_summary %>%
  # We group by location and lactation group
  gt(groupname_col = c("location_event", "lact_group")) %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Economic Impact: Mastitis Status by Lactation",
    subtitle = "Days in Milk (DIM) from Calving to Exit or Last Event"
  ) %>%
  
  # Format Numbers
  fmt_number(columns = Avg_Lactation_Length, decimals = 0) %>%
  fmt_number(columns = Culling_Rate, decimals = 1, pattern = "{x}%") %>%
  
  # Column Labels
  cols_label(
    mast_status = "Mastitis Category",
    Cow_Count = "Total Cows",
    Culling_Rate = "Culling Rate (%)",
    Avg_Lactation_Length = "Avg Days in Herd"
  ) %>%
  
  # FIXED COLORING: Expanded domain to 100 to fix the grey color for MV Dairy
  data_color(
    columns = Culling_Rate,
    direction = "column",
    palette = c("white", "orange", "red"),
    domain = c(0, 100) 
  ) %>%
  
  # Nanoplots
  cols_nanoplot(
    columns = Avg_Lactation_Length,
    plot_type = "bar",
    new_col_name = "DIM_Plot",
    options = nanoplot_options(data_bar_fill = "darkgreen")
  ) %>%
  
  # Aesthetics
  tab_options(
    row_group.background.color = "#f4f4f4",
    row_group.font.weight = "bold",
    table.width = pct(100)
  ) %>%
  cols_align(align = "center", columns = everything())

# Display the table
mast_econ_table_lact
```

```{r}
#|label: heatmap mast

mast_heatmap <- ggplot(mast_econ_summary, aes(x = lact_group, y = mast_status, fill = Culling_Rate)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.5) + 
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%", Culling_Rate)), 
            color = "black", size = 3.5, fontface = "bold") + 
  facet_wrap(~location_event, ncol = 1) + 
  
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("white", "orange", "red"),
    values = scales::rescale(c(0, 30, 100)), 
    name = "Culling Rate (%)",
    limits = c(0, 100)
  ) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Mastitis Economic Risk Heat Map",
    subtitle = "Culling Rate (%) by Lactation Group and Mastitis Status",
    x = "Lactation Group",
    y = "Mastitis Status"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "#eeeeee"), # Grey background for Dairy labels
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 16),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 12),
    legend.position = "right"
  )

# Display the heatmap
print(mast_heatmap)

```
